about.title: About Prime Trust Finance
about.subtitle: Building a better future through trusted financial partnerships
about.story1: Prime Trust Finance was founded in 2018 with a vision to bridge the gap between traditional banking and the real financial needs of individuals and businesses across Rwanda. What started as a small community-focused lender has grown into a trusted financial institution serving thousands of customers nationwide.
about.story2: Our journey has been driven by a deep understanding of the local market and a commitment to financial inclusion. We believe that everyone deserves access to quality financial services, regardless of their background or circumstances.
about.story3: Today, Prime Trust Finance offers a comprehensive range of products including savings accounts, loans, cards, money transfers, and bill payments. We combine technology with personal service to deliver the best banking experience for our customers.
about.missionText: To empower our customers with accessible, innovative, and responsible financial solutions that transform lives and build sustainable communities.
about.visionText: To be the most trusted and innovative financial partner, driving prosperity and financial inclusion across Africa.

faqPage.title: FAQ
faqPage.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our services

questions.q1Answer:
• I'm buying a house
  • Buying a new plot
  • I'm Buying a car
questions.q2: How can I contact customer support?
questions.q2Answer: You can reach our customer support team by calling 4455 from Monday to Friday, 9AM to 5PM, and Saturday from 9AM to 1PM. You can also email us at info@primetrust.com or visit any of our branches.
questions.q3Answer: Loan amounts vary depending on the type of loan and your creditworthiness. Personal loans start from RWF 500,000 up to RWF 50,000,000. Business loans can go up to RWF 500,000,000. Visit our Loans page or contact us for a personalized assessment.
questions.q4: What documents are needed to open an account?
questions.q4Answer: To open an account, you will need a valid government-issued ID (National ID or Passport), proof of residence (utility bill or bank statement), passport-sized photographs, and a tax identification number. Additional documents may be required for business accounts.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we protect your personal information
privacy.heroDesc: We are committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information.
privacy.section1: We collect personal information that you provide to us, including name, address, email, phone number, financial information, and identification documents. We also collect information about your transactions and account activity.
privacy.section2: We use your information to provide banking services, process transactions, verify your identity, comply with legal requirements, and improve our services. We may also use your information to send you marketing communications with your consent.
privacy.section3: We do not sell your personal information. We may share your information with trusted third-party service providers who assist us in delivering our services, subject to strict confidentiality agreements.
privacy.section4: We employ industry-standard security measures to protect your information, including encryption, firewalls, and secure data centers. We regularly review and update our security practices.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: Terms and conditions for using our services
terms.heroDesc: Please read these terms carefully before using our services.
terms.section1: By opening an account with Prime Trust Finance, you agree to provide accurate and complete information. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.
terms.section2: Our electronic banking services are provided for your convenience. We strive to maintain uninterrupted access but cannot guarantee availability at all times. We are not liable for losses resulting from unauthorized access to your account.
terms.section3: Fees for banking services are outlined in our fee schedule, which may be updated from time to time. We will provide notice of fee changes as required by applicable regulations.
terms.section4: Our liability for any claim arising from our services is limited as set forth in these terms. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

list.branchDesc: We are excited to announce the opening of our new branch in Musanze District, bringing our services closer to you. Visit us for account opening, loans, and more.
list.mobileDesc: Our mobile app now supports Ikofi MoMo integration and real-time transaction alerts in Kinyarwanda, English, and French.
list.ratesDesc: We've updated our savings and fixed deposit rates. Enjoy up to 8.5% p.a. on savings accounts and 12% p.a. on fixed deposits.
list.holidayDesc: Our branches will be closed on Independence Day (July 1) and Liberation Day (July 4). Mobile and internet banking remain available 24/7.
list.diasporaDesc: Introducing tailored banking solutions for Rwandans living abroad. Open accounts remotely, send remittances, and invest back home with ease.
